task_completion.description: Revoke excessive privileges from DEV_USER and REPORT_USER2.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for SQL execution in a database client (like SQL Developer or SQL*Plus) showing 'REVOKE DBA FROM DEV_USER;' and 'REVOKE CREATE TABLE, SELECT ANY TABLE FROM REPORT_USER2;' with a success message.

task_completion.description: Revoke privileges from APP_USER and lock the LEGACY_USER account.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for SQL execution showing 'REVOKE RESOURCE, UNLIMITED TABLESPACE FROM APP_USER;' and 'ALTER USER LEGACY_USER ACCOUNT LOCK;' with a success message.

task_completion.description: Ensure ANALYST_USER has only CREATE SESSION and CREATE VIEW privileges.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for SQL execution that revokes excess privileges from ANALYST_USER, leaving only CREATE SESSION and CREATE VIEW, or explicitly granting only those two privileges after a full revoke.

task_completion.description: Create the Oracle Unified Audit policy named PRIVILEGE_ESCALATION_AUDIT.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the execution of a CREATE AUDIT POLICY statement for 'PRIVILEGE_ESCALATION_AUDIT' covering the exact privileges: GRANT ANY PRIVILEGE, CREATE USER, DROP USER, ALTER USER, and CREATE ROLE.

task_completion.description: Enable the newly created audit policy.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the execution of the SQL command 'AUDIT POLICY PRIVILEGE_ESCALATION_AUDIT;' with a success message indicating the policy was enabled.

task_completion.description: Export the security remediation report documenting the findings and fixes.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a text editor or terminal showing a file being saved to the exact path '/home/ga/Documents/exports/security_remediation.txt'. The file contents should document the 5 accounts and the remediation actions taken.

integrity.description: Verify the agent actually interacted with the Oracle database to apply the changes.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show a real database client (e.g., Oracle SQL Developer, SQL*Plus in terminal) with successful execution outputs (e.g., 'Revoke succeeded', 'User altered', 'Audit policy created'), rather than just typing commands into a text file without running them.

integrity.description: Verify the remediation report is genuinely written and not just a copy-paste of the task prompt.
integrity.visual_evidence: The text in '/home/ga/Documents/exports/security_remediation.txt' should be formatted as a report detailing the findings and fixes, rather than an exact verbatim copy of the prompt instructions.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not just echo success messages to the terminal to simulate completion.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for actual SQL client prompts (like 'SQL>') or GUI execution results, rather than basic shell 'echo' commands printing fake success messages.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target users to remediate: DEV_USER, REPORT_USER2, ANALYST_USER, APP_USER, LEGACY_USER. The audit policy must be exactly named 'PRIVILEGE_ESCALATION_AUDIT'. The final report must be saved to '/home/ga/Documents/exports/security_remediation.txt'. The agent should use the system database credentials (user: 'system', password: 'OraclePassword123') to execute the database changes.
